name: xmemory__remote-mcp
description: Persistent structured memory for AI agents — save, recall, and update facts, decisions, people, and projects across sessions. xmemory is a first-party memory store: it holds the data you explicitly save to your xmemory instance, in xmemory's own backend. It does NOT read the assistant's built-in memory, your past chat history, or your files, email, or cloud drives — it only stores and returns what is written to this instance.

oauth_config.name: xmemory MCP
oauth_config.description: xmemory MCP server (OAuth 2.1 + PKCE, dynamic client registration)

tools.name: write_async
tools.description: Preferred write path — save create/update/delete intent in natural language; returns immediately

tools.name: write
tools.description: Synchronous write; use only when you must read the same data back in the same turn

tools.name: write_status
tools.description: Diagnostic only — check once whether a specific async write landed

tools.name: read
tools.description: Query the instance in natural language (lookups, aggregations, listings, traversals)

tools.name: get_instance_id
tools.description: Return the instance ID for the current session

tools.name: review_suggestions
tools.description: See schema improvements proposed from your read/write traffic

tools.name: decide_suggestions
tools.description: Accept / reject / defer schema improvements proposals (bulk)

tools.name: apply_pending_decisions
tools.description: Commit accepted proposals as a migration
